texto: I think the difficulty lies in the immeasurable vanity of the human adult, particularly the pedagogical adult,… which does not permit him to recognize as good any tendency in children to fly in the face of his conceptions of a correct human being; to recognize that may be here is something highly desirable, to be encourage, rather than destroyed as pernicious…. [Y]our teacher has usually well-defined conceptions of what men and women have to be. And if a boy is too lively, too noisy, too restless, too curious, to suit the concept, he must be trimmed and subdued.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Voltairine de Cleyre sobre enfrentar incertezas com ação. A afirmação “I think the difficulty lies in the immeasurable vanity of the human adult, particularly the pedag…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a educacao, education, educational-philosophy, útil em momentos de termino e educational-philosophy.

texto: It is the city that is wrong, and its creations can never be right; they may be improved; they can never be what they should.... Until the whole atrocious system of herding working people in close-built cities, by way of making them serviceable cogwheels in the capitalistic machine for grinding out rent and profit, comes to an end, the physical education of children will remain at best a pathetic compromise.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Voltairine de Cleyre sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “It is the city that is wrong, and its creations can never be right; they may be improved; they ca…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a educacao, education, reflexao, útil em momentos de termino e trabalho.

Until the whole atrocious system of herding working people in close-built cities, by way of making them serviceable cogwheels in the capitalistic machine for grinding out rent and profit, comes to an end, the physical education of children will remain at best a pathetic compromise.

texto: We dabble in many things; but the one great real idea of our age, not copied from any other, not pretended, not raised to life by any conjuration, is the Much Making of Things – not the making of beautiful things, not the joy of spending living energy in creative work; rather the shameless, merciless driving and over-driving, wasting and draining of the last bit of energy, only to produce heaps and heaps of things – things ugly, things harmful, things useless, and at the best largely unnecessary.
explicacao: Citação de Voltairine de Cleyre sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “We dabble in many things; but the one great real idea of our age, not copied from any other, not …” articula vida, capitalism, materialism em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como dia-chuvoso e termino.

texto: I die, as I have lived, a free spirit, an Anarchist, owing no allegiance to rulers, heavenly or earthly.
explicacao: Citação de Voltairine de Cleyre sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “I die, as I have lived, a free spirit, an Anarchist, owing no allegiance to rulers, heavenly or e…” articula fe, anarchy, atheism em registro de fe, com relevância para contextos como atheism e religion.
